title: Desmophyllum pinnatum
intro: Desmophyllum pinnatum, also known as the coral tree, is a type of marine coral plant that belongs to the order Scleractinia. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against infections, reduce inflammation, and combat oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Desmophyllum pinnatum include compounds such as brominated pyrroles, halogenated furanones, and other halogenated compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Desmophyllum pinnatum is often used to make herbal preparations, including extracts and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Desmophyllum pinnatum is classified as a species of coral within the family Scleractinia. It belongs to the genus Desmophyllum, which is a part of the phylum Cnidaria. This coral species is also known as the pinnate star coral due to its distinctive star-shaped morphology. Desmophyllum pinnatum is commonly found in tropical and subtropical waters around the world. The classification of Desmophyllum pinnatum is further refined at the species level, reflecting its unique characteristics within the coral kingdom.
